sentence_reviews.text: Curriculum and mixture schedules control which examples, domains, difficulties, or modalities are sampled at each phase of training.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Existing ledger for active_curriculum_learning cites AIMA active learning and learning-from-examples topics; Hoffmann arXiv abstract lines 39-42 discusses model size, number of tokens, training data, and compute-optimal token allocation. No inspected source directly covers multimodal mixture schedules.
sentence_reviews.notes: Example/domain/difficulty scheduling is defensible from active/curriculum and data-token allocation evidence; modalities are broader.

sentence_reviews.text: combined with Active learning & curriculum learning
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Existing source_sentence_ledger entries for active_curriculum_learning cite AIMA Chapter 19 learning from examples and active learning plus PML data/validation/sampling topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Active learning is directly supported; curriculum learning and mixture schedules are synthesized from data selection/order.

sentence_reviews.text: Results are sensitive to data quality, hyperparameters, compute budget, random variation, and evaluation contamination. Its direct dependencies include Active learning & curriculum learning, Data collection, curation & filtering.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.
